author: PC Gamer
text:
No, Eric Barone is not adding infidelity to Stardew Valley, although he did briefly consider letting you ruin marriages, to Grandpa's deep disappointment
Some idle musing about the nature of morality in sandbox games has spiraled out of control.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Capcom says it's eyeing Devil May Cry, Ace Attorney, Dragon's Dogma for 'sequels, remakes, ports, etc.', and I'll gladly take the excuse to get my hopes up
Dragon's Dogma mentioned!

author: PC Gamer
text:
Doom is 10 years old today, and you can pick it up for just $2 on Steam
This is not a deal to be missed.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bungie is turning Marathon's loot dial to 11 ahead of its first wipe, opening up Cryo Archive 24/7
The time has come to actually use all the good stuff in your vault.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Turn-based tactics game Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus 2 will release next week to coincide with a Warhammer Skulls event stacked with reveals
Some of them will be grim. Some of them will be dark.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Subnautica 2 devs say it's 'bigger and more polished' than any of the studio's previous early access launches
Unknown Worlds is eager to collaborate with its community once again.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ubisoft's leak-a-thon continues with a dump of Assassin's Creed Hexe info that hints at the return of a familiar 'old man'
Sadly, there's no hint at a release date in the leak.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel is back in F1 as it officially partners with last year's winners McLaren to provide the team with heaps of chips
Gotta go fast.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Overwatch's OG mode has made me realise how far this game has come and how good we have it right now
Why is everything and everyone so bloody slow?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Netflix misspells the name of Devil May Cry antagonist Vergil on a t-shirt, and it's honestly one of the more understandable typos they could've made
You say Vergil, I say Virgil, you say tomato, I say…

author: PC Gamer
text:
The US has approved the sale of Nvidia H200 chips to 10 Chinese firms, but sources say they're still waiting for the go-ahead from China itself
Nvidia said it had 0% market share in China mere months ago.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6 launch times and release date in your region
The Horizon Festival's going to Japan, finally.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AMD bringing FSR 4.1 ML-powered upscaling to RX 7000-series in July, RX 6000-series in 2027
Modders and Sony proved it possible. Now AMD is making good with official support.

author: PC Gamer
text:
IO Interactive has forgiven me for calling 007 a smug tool
I am absolved of my sins until at least my next article.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Summer Game Fest 2026 schedule and all the other summer showcases full of gaming announcements you won't want to miss
Keep up with when to watch this summer's lineup of major gaming announcements.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Amazon reportedly had a studio pivot away from a game 'everyone was excited by' to make something with more AI stuffed in it, then laid them all off anyway
Beyond parody.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The PC Gaming Show returns with both new and familiar faces to host our summer look at some of the coolest upcoming games
Four, count 'em, four, great hosts.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Bambu Labs, go f*** yourself': 3D printing company currently under fire for reportedly blocking 3rd parties from its software, legal threats and drama aplenty
How open is open source code, really?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Even if you have an RTX 5090, I don't recommend using ray tracing in Forza Horizon 6. Not because of the performance hit, simply because it's just not worth using
If I'm going to burn off some fps, then give my eyes something to go dizzy over.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6 PC performance analysis: Heavy on your CPU, with uninspiring ray tracing, but at least it all runs very nicely
From handhelds to old gaming PCs, pretty much anything will run the new Forza.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Forza Horizon 6 review
Riding a well-worn track.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AOC's new 34-inch gaming monitor gets the same sizzling 5th Gen QD-OLED panel tech as our current favorite ultrawide but it's a whole lot cheaper
Still not exactly a bargain, but at least it's not sold out.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'It's Temu Google Stadia': Vtuber showcases backseat gaming tool while playing Dark Souls 2 for the very first time
Strap in.
